info.title: Alien Manager API
info.description: Control plane for Alien applications. Manages deployments, releases, commands, and telemetry.

get.summary: Get command payload (params and response) from KV
get.description:
Returns the raw params and response data stored in the manager's KV store.
Returns 404 if neither params nor response exist for this command.

put.summary: Store command payload data (params and/or response) directly into KV.
put.description:
Bypasses the command registry — useful for populating demo data or
migrating payload data. Does not validate command existence or state.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Force delete without running cleanup (immediately removes record)

200.description: Agent sync response with optional target state
Returns the identity of the authenticated caller

currentState.description:
Current deployment state as reported by the agent.
When present, the manager updates the deployment record to reflect
the agent's progress (status, stack_state, etc.).

commandsUrl.description:
Public URL for the commands API. Cloud-deployed functions use this
to poll for pending commands instead of the agent's local sync URL.

size.description: Size of the body in bytes

storageGetRequest.description: Pre-signed request for retrieving the body (for deployments)

storagePutUsed.description: Indicates storage upload was used for response submission

BodySpec.description: Body specification supporting inline and storage modes

CommandPayloadResponse.description: Payload response containing params and response data from KV

CommandState.description: Command states in the Commands protocol lifecycle

CreateCommandRequest.description: Request to create a new command

storageUpload.description: Storage upload info (only for storage mode)

CustomCertificateConfig.description: Platform-specific certificate references for custom domains.

CustomDomainConfig.description: Custom domain configuration for a single resource.

domain.description: Fully qualified domain name to use.

DeploymentModel.description: Deployment model: how updates are delivered to the remote environment.

DomainSettings.description:
Domain configuration for the stack.

When `custom_domains` is set, the specified resources use customer-provided
domains and certificates. Otherwise, Alien auto-generates domains.

customDomains.description: Custom domain configuration per resource ID.

targetResources.description: Target resource patterns (null = all resources, Some = wildcard patterns)

value.description: Variable value (decrypted - deployment has access to decryption keys)

ErrorResponse.description: Error response wrapper for API endpoints

HeartbeatsMode.description: How heartbeat health checks are handled.

maxLeases.description: Maximum number of leases to acquire

leases.description: Acquired leases (empty array if none available)

oneOf.description:
Use the cloud provider's default VPC/network.

Designed for fast dev/test provisioning. No isolated VPC is created, so there
is nothing to wait for or clean up. VMs receive ephemeral public IPs for internet
access — no NAT gateway is provisioned.

- **AWS**: Discovers the account's default VPC. Subnets are public with auto-assigned IPs.
- **GCP**: Discovers the project's `default` network and regional subnet. Instance
  templates include an `AccessConfig` to assign an ephemeral external IP.
- **Azure**: Azure has no default VNet, so one is created along with a NAT Gateway.
  VMs stay private and use NAT for egress.

Not recommended for production. Use `Create` instead.

Create a new isolated VPC/VNet with a managed NAT gateway.

All networking infrastructure is provisioned by Alien and cleaned up on delete.
VMs use private IPs only; all outbound traffic routes through the NAT gateway.

Recommended for production deployments.

cidr.description:
VPC/VNet CIDR block. If not specified, auto-generated from stack ID
to reduce conflicts (e.g., "10.{hash}.0.0/16").

oneOf.description:
Use an existing VPC (AWS).

Alien validates the references but creates no networking infrastructure.
The customer is responsible for routing and egress (NAT, proxy, VPN, etc.).

public_subnet_ids.description: IDs of public subnets (required for public ingress)

security_group_ids.description: Optional security group IDs to use

vpc_id.description: The ID of the existing VPC

oneOf.description:
Use an existing VPC (GCP).

Alien validates the references but creates no networking infrastructure.
The customer is responsible for routing and egress (Cloud NAT, proxy, VPN, etc.).

network_name.description: The name of the existing VPC network

subnet_name.description: The name of the subnet to use

oneOf.description:
Use an existing VNet (Azure).

Alien validates the references but creates no networking infrastructure.
The customer is responsible for routing and egress (NAT Gateway, proxy, VPN, etc.).

private_subnet_name.description: Name of the private subnet within the VNet

public_subnet_name.description: Name of the public subnet within the VNet

vnet_resource_id.description: The full resource ID of the existing VNet

NetworkSettings.description:
Network configuration for the stack.

Controls how VPC/VNet networking is provisioned. Users configure this in
`StackSettings`; the Network resource itself is auto-generated by preflights.

## Egress policy

Container cluster VMs are configured for egress based on the mode:

- `UseDefault` → VMs get ephemeral public IPs (no NAT is provisioned)
- `Create` → VMs use private IPs; Alien provisions a NAT gateway for outbound access
- `ByoVpc*` / `ByoVnet*` → no public IPs assigned; customer manages egress

For production workloads, use `Create`. For fast dev/test iteration, `UseDefault` is
sufficient. For environments with existing VPCs, use the appropriate `ByoVpc*` variant.

PresignedOperation.description: The type of operation a presigned request performs

PresignedRequest.description:
A presigned request that can be serialized, stored, and executed later.
Hides implementation details for different storage backends.

backend.description: The storage backend this request targets

path.description: The path this request operates on

oneOf.description: HTTP-based request (AWS S3, GCP GCS, Azure Blob)

PresignedRequestBackend.description: Storage backend representation for different presigned request types

nativeImageHost.description:
Native image registry host for Lambda/Cloud Run.
Returned so push clients can set it on their local DeploymentConfig.

maxInlineBytes.description: Maximum response body size that can be submitted inline

storageUploadRequest.description: Pre-signed request for uploading large response bodies

StackByPlatform.description:
The release API accepts stacks keyed by platform.
Only one platform stack needs to be present.

StackSettings.description:
User-customizable deployment settings specified at deploy time.

These settings are provided by the customer via CloudFormation parameters,
Terraform attributes, CLI flags, or Helm values. They customize how the
deployment runs and what capabilities are enabled.

**Key distinction**: StackSettings is user-customizable, while ManagementConfig
is platform-derived (from the Manager's ServiceAccount).

deploymentModel.description:
Deployment model: push (Manager) or pull (Agent).
Default: Push.
- Push: Manager drives updates. For cloud platforms, requires cross-account
  credentials established during initial setup. For push-mode local
  deployments (currently `alien dev`), the manager has direct access —
  no bootstrap needed.
- Pull: Agent in the target environment drives updates via polling.
  Required for Kubernetes and remote local deployments.

externalBindings.description:
External bindings for pre-existing infrastructure.
Allows using existing resources (MinIO, Redis, shared Container Apps
Environment, etc.) instead of having Alien provision them.
Required for Kubernetes platform, optional for cloud platforms.

heartbeats.description:
How heartbeat health checks are handled.
- off: No heartbeat permissions
- on: Heartbeat enabled (default)

network.description:
Network configuration for the stack (VPC/VNet settings).
If `None`, an isolated VPC with NAT is auto-created when the stack has resources
that require networking (e.g., containers). Set explicitly to customize:
`UseDefault` for the provider's default network (fast, dev/test only),
`Create` for an isolated VPC with managed NAT (production), or `ByoVpc*`
to reference an existing customer-managed VPC.

telemetry.description:
How telemetry (logs, metrics, traces) is handled.
- off: No telemetry permissions
- auto: Telemetry flows automatically (default)
- approval-required: Telemetry waits for explicit approval

updates.description:
How updates are delivered.
- auto: Updates deploy automatically (default)
- approval-required: Updates wait for explicit approval

putRequest.description: Pre-signed request for uploading command params

StorePayloadRequest.description:
Request to store payload data directly in KV by command_id.

This bypasses the normal command lifecycle (create → dispatch → respond)
and writes params/response directly into KV. Used by the demo service
to populate payload data for commands created outside the command flow.

SubmitResponseRequest.description: Request to submit a command response (from deployment)

TelemetryMode.description: How telemetry (logs, metrics, traces) is handled.

UpdatesMode.description: How updates are delivered to the deployment.

UploadCompleteRequest.description: Request to mark upload as complete
